_comment: Cygnet UI config for the Myanmar Open Wordnet.
title: Myanmar Open Wordnet
name: MOW

main.description: The main MOW lexical database, containing synsets, entries, senses, and definitions.

provenance.description: Provenance records tracing every data point back to its source.

about.intro:
The Myanmar Open Wordnet (MOW) is a freely-available semantic dictionary of the Myanmar/Burmese language, inspired by Princeton WordNet and the Global WordNet Grid. It is part of the Open Multilingual Wordnet (Bond and Foster, 2013).

Source code on GitHub. Licensed under CC BY 4.0.
about.citation: If you use the Myanmar Open Wordnet, please cite Wang Wenji (n.d.). The interface is Cygnet (Maudslay & Bond, 2026).
about.languageData: The Myanmar Open Wordnet covers Myanmar/Burmese (my), linked to English via the Collaborative Interlingual Index.  We also show Myanmar data from the Tokyo University of Studies Basic Vocabulary and English from the Open English wordnet.

publications: Rowan Hall Maudslay & Francis Bond (2026). Cygnet: Refactoring the Open Multilingual Wordnet. In Proceedings of the 15th International Conference on Language Resources and Evaluation (LREC 2026). (to appear)

Francis Bond & Ryan Foster (2013). Linking and extending an open multilingual wordnet. In Proceedings of the 51st Annual Meeting of the Association for Computational Linguistics (ACL 2013). Sofia. pp. 1352–1362.
